file_insights.changes: Added 8 new serializers for organization and user management operations
key_additions: OrganizationListSerializer - Summary organization data with user_count
OrganizationDetailSerializer - Full organization data with user and branch counts
OrganizationCreateSerializer - Organization creation with auto-generated slug
UserListSerializer - Optimized user listing with full_name and is_active_user
UserDetailSerializer - Comprehensive user data with organization info and roles
UserCreateSerializer - User creation with password validation and role assignment
UserUpdateSerializer - Update user profile with self-suspension prevention
file_insights.pattern: Tiered serializer approach (List/Detail/Create/Update) for resource management

file_insights.changes: Restructured URL patterns with Router-based resource endpoints
key_additions: DefaultRouter instance for organizations and users ViewSets
file_insights.pattern: RESTful router-based URL structure for scalable endpoint management

file_insights.changes: Implemented ViewSets for Organization and User resource management
key_additions: OrganizationViewSet with list, create, retrieve, update, destroy actions
UserViewSet with list, create, retrieve, update, destroy actions
file_insights.pattern: DRY ViewSet implementation with consistent CRUD operations

patterns_discovered.description: Different serializers for List (minimal), Detail (comprehensive), Create (validation), and Update (restricted fields) operations
patterns_discovered.benefit: Optimizes API responses by avoiding over-fetching data while maintaining validation strictness

patterns_discovered.description: OrganizationCreateSerializer automatically generates URL-safe slug from name with collision handling
patterns_discovered.benefit: User-friendly identifiers without requiring manual slug input

patterns_discovered.description: get_user_count(), get_branch_count(), get_roles() methods for dynamic field computation
patterns_discovered.benefit: Avoids database denormalization while providing aggregated data

patterns_discovered.description: DefaultRouter automatically generates standard CRUD endpoints for ViewSets
patterns_discovered.benefit: Reduces boilerplate URL patterns and ensures consistency across resources

patterns_discovered.description: Filters use deleted_at__isnull=True to exclude soft-deleted records from counts
patterns_discovered.benefit: Preserves data while logically removing it from API responses

patterns_discovered.description: Field-level (validate_slug), object-level (validate), and business logic validation (confirm_password, self-suspension check)
patterns_discovered.benefit: Comprehensive error handling with clear user feedback

gotchas_discovered.issue: Requires explicit confirm_password field separate from password field
gotchas_discovered.solution: Both fields are write_only; confirm_password is validated and removed before user creation

gotchas_discovered.issue: Role assignment silently fails if role_id is invalid (pass statement)
gotchas_discovered.risk: User is created without role if invalid role_id provided; no validation error raised

gotchas_discovered.issue: UserUpdateSerializer prevents user from suspending their own account but requires request context
gotchas_discovered.risk: If context['request'] is missing, validation is skipped

gotchas_discovered.issue: OrganizationCreateSerializer uses simple counter suffix; doesn't handle very high collision scenarios efficiently
gotchas_discovered.risk: Potential performance issue with many organizations sharing same base slug

gotchas_discovered.issue: UserDetailSerializer uses source='organization.name' which can return None if user has no organization
gotchas_discovered.risk: N+1 query problem if not optimized with select_related()

gotchas_discovered.issue: UserCreateSerializer validates and normalizes email to lowercase but doesn't enforce this at model level
gotchas_discovered.risk: Inconsistency if user created directly via model instead of serializer

approach_outcome.completion_level: Complete API implementation for core organization and user management
key_accomplishments: Established comprehensive serializer hierarchy for all CRUD operations
Implemented ViewSets with soft-delete support and permission checking
Created RESTful URL structure using DefaultRouter for scalability
Added computed fields for aggregated data (user_count, branch_count, roles)
Implemented validation at multiple levels with clear error messages
Maintained backward compatibility with existing minimal serializers
approach_outcome.testing_readiness: Code follows DRF conventions and best practices; ready for unit and integration tests
approach_outcome.documentation: Comprehensive docstrings for all serializers and clear URL structure documentation

recommendations.recommendation: Replace silent role assignment failure with explicit error handling
recommendations.implementation: Raise ValidationError if role_id is provided but invalid instead of silently passing
Override get_queryset() to include select_related('organization') for UserDetailSerializer and prefetch_related('users') for OrganizationDetailSerializer

recommendations.recommendation: Add UniqueValidator to email field in UserCreateSerializer
recommendations.implementation: Use DRF's UniqueValidator instead of custom validation for consistency

recommendations.recommendation: Improve slug collision handling with UUID-based fallback
recommendations.implementation: After counter exceeds threshold (e.g., 100), append UUID suffix instead of continuing counter

recommendations.recommendation: Ensure request context is always available in UserUpdateSerializer
recommendations.implementation: Add explicit check in validate_status() and raise ValidationError if request missing

recommendations.recommendation: Add test coverage for all serializer validation methods
recommendations.implementation: Create test files for edge cases: duplicate email, password mismatch, invalid role, self-suspension

recommendations.recommendation: Consider adding pagination configuration to ViewSets
recommendations.implementation: Add PageNumberPagination or CursorPagination to handle large user/organization lists
